name: functions
context: Deploy event-driven serverless functions on Google Cloud Functions — HTTP triggers, Pub/Sub, GCS events, and Cloud Eventarc
info: This skill covers Google Cloud Functions for serverless compute: writing functions in Python, Node.js, Go, or Java; deploying with HTTP triggers or event triggers (Cloud Storage, Pub/Sub, Firestore, Eventarc); configuring memory, timeout, concurrency, and environment variables; and managing function versions and traffic splitting. Cloud Functions v2 (built on Cloud Run) is the current generation, offering longer timeouts (up to 60 minutes), larger instances (up to 32 GiB RAM), concurrency, and Eventarc integration. Agents use this skill to deploy webhooks, process file uploads, handle queue messages, and run scheduled jobs. See [[gcp/iam]] for required roles (roles/cloudfunctions.developer) and [[gcp/gcloud]] for CLI setup.
triggers: create a serverless function on gcp
set up an http cloud function
run serverless code on google cloud
body:
# Google Cloud Functions

Cloud Functions is Google's serverless compute platform for event-driven code. Write a function, deploy it, and Google handles the servers, scaling, and availability. Cloud Functions v2 (2nd gen) is built on Cloud Run and is the recommended version.

## Cloud Functions v1 vs v2

| Feature | v1 (1st gen) | v2 (2nd gen) |
|---------|-------------|-------------|
| Max timeout | 9 min | 60 min |
| Max memory | 8 GiB | 32 GiB |
| Max instances | 3,000 | 1,000 (configurable) |
| Concurrency | 1 per instance | Up to 1,000 per instance |
| Event sources | Native GCS/Pub/Sub | Eventarc (90+ sources) |
| Min instances | Yes | Yes |
| Traffic splitting | No | Yes |
| Built on | Custom | Cloud Run |

## HTTP Function (Python)

```python
# main.py
import functions_framework
from flask import jsonify

@functions_framework.http
def hello(request):
    """HTTP Cloud Function."""
    name = request.args.get("name", "World")
    return jsonify({"message": f"Hello, {name}!"})
```

```txt
# requirements.txt
functions-framework==3.*
```

```bash
# Deploy v2 HTTP function
gcloud functions deploy hello \
  --gen2 \
  --runtime=python312 \
  --region=us-central1 \
  --source=. \
  --entry-point=hello \
  --trigger-http \
  --allow-unauthenticated \
  --memory=256Mi \
  --timeout=60s
```

## HTTP Function (Node.js)

```javascript
// index.js
const functions = require('@google-cloud/functions-framework');

functions.http('hello', (req, res) => {
  const name = req.query.name || 'World';
  res.json({ message: `Hello, ${name}!` });
});
```

```json
// package.json
{
  "dependencies": {
    "@google-cloud/functions-framework": "^3.0.0"
  }
}
```

```bash
# Deploy v2 HTTP function (Node.js)
gcloud functions deploy hello \
  --gen2 \
  --runtime=nodejs20 \
  --region=us-central1 \
  --source=. \
  --entry-point=hello \
  --trigger-http \
  --allow-unauthenticated
```

## Cloud Storage Event Function

```python
# main.py
import functions_framework
from cloudevents.http import CloudEvent

@functions_framework.cloud_event
def process_upload(cloud_event: CloudEvent):
    """Triggered by a GCS object finalize event."""
    data = cloud_event.data
    bucket = data["bucket"]
    name = data["name"]
    print(f"Processing file: gs://{bucket}/{name}")
    print(f"Content type: {data.get('contentType', 'unknown')}")
    print(f"Size: {data.get('size', 'unknown')} bytes")
```

```bash
# Deploy GCS event function
gcloud functions deploy process-upload \
  --gen2 \
  --runtime=python312 \
  --region=us-central1 \
  --source=. \
  --entry-point=process_upload \
  --trigger-event-filters="type=google.cloud.storage.object.v1.finalized" \
  --trigger-event-filters="bucket=my-uploads-bucket"
```

## Pub/Sub Event Function

```python
# main.py
import base64
import functions_framework
from cloudevents.http import CloudEvent

@functions_framework.cloud_event
def process_message(cloud_event: CloudEvent):
    """Triggered by a Pub/Sub message."""
    data = base64.b64decode(cloud_event.data["message"]["data"]).decode("utf-8")
    print(f"Received message: {data}")
```

```bash
# Deploy Pub/Sub function
gcloud functions deploy process-message \
  --gen2 \
  --runtime=python312 \
  --region=us-central1 \
  --source=. \
  --entry-point=process_message \
  --trigger-topic=my-topic
```

## Function Management

```bash
# List functions
gcloud functions list --gen2 --region=us-central1

# Describe a function
gcloud functions describe hello --gen2 --region=us-central1

# View logs
gcloud functions logs read hello --gen2 --region=us-central1 --limit=50

# Delete a function
gcloud functions delete hello --gen2 --region=us-central1 --quiet

# Set environment variables
gcloud functions deploy hello \
  --gen2 \
  --runtime=python312 \
  --region=us-central1 \
  --source=. \
  --entry-point=hello \
  --trigger-http \
  --set-env-vars="DATABASE_URL=postgres://...,API_KEY=secret123"

# Set secrets from Secret Manager
gcloud functions deploy hello \
  --gen2 \
  --runtime=python312 \
  --region=us-central1 \
  --source=. \
  --entry-point=hello \
  --trigger-http \
  --set-secrets="API_KEY=projects/my-project/secrets/api-key:latest"
```

## Local Development

```bash
# Python — local dev with functions-framework
pip install functions-framework
functions-framework --target=hello --port=8080 --debug

# Node.js — local dev
npx @google-cloud/functions-framework --target=hello --port=8080

# Test locally
curl http://localhost:8080?name=Agent
```

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|----------|
| `GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT` | Project ID (auto-set in Cloud Functions) |
| `FUNCTION_TARGET` | Entry point function name |
| `FUNCTION_SIGNATURE_TYPE` | http or cloudevent |
| `PORT` | HTTP port (default 8080) |

sections.title: HTTP functions
sections.body: HTTP functions respond to HTTP requests at a URL endpoint. Use `@functions_framework.http` (Python) or `functions.http()` (Node.js). Deploy with `--trigger-http`. Add `--allow-unauthenticated` for public access; omit it to require IAM authentication (Bearer token). The function URL is `https://REGION-PROJECT_ID.cloudfunctions.net/FUNCTION_NAME`. HTTP functions are ideal for webhooks, REST APIs, and web endpoints.

sections.title: Event-driven functions
sections.body: Event functions trigger on Cloud Events from GCS, Pub/Sub, Firestore, Firebase Auth, and 90+ Eventarc sources. Use `@functions_framework.cloud_event` (Python) or `functions.cloudEvent()` (Node.js). Deploy with `--trigger-event-filters` for v2 or `--trigger-event` for v1. The CloudEvent object contains `data` (event payload), `source`, `type`, and `subject`. GCS events: `google.cloud.storage.object.v1.finalized`, `.deleted`, `.archived`, `.metadataUpdated`.

sections.title: Function configuration
sections.body: Key settings: `--memory` (128Mi to 32Gi), `--timeout` (up to 3600s for v2), `--min-instances` (avoid cold starts), `--max-instances` (limit scaling), `--concurrency` (v2 only, requests per instance). Set environment variables with `--set-env-vars` or secrets with `--set-secrets` (reads from Secret Manager). Runtimes: python312, python311, nodejs20, nodejs18, go122, java21.

sections.title: Local development and testing
sections.body: Use `functions-framework` to run functions locally. Python: `pip install functions-framework && functions-framework --target=FUNCTION --port=8080`. Node.js: `npx @google-cloud/functions-framework --target=FUNCTION --port=8080`. Test with curl or any HTTP client. The functions framework replicates the production runtime for both HTTP and CloudEvent functions. Use `--debug` for auto-reload on code changes.

sections.title: Deployment and management
sections.body: Deploy with `gcloud functions deploy NAME --gen2 --runtime=RUNTIME --region=REGION --source=. --entry-point=FUNCTION`. View logs with `gcloud functions logs read NAME`. Delete with `gcloud functions delete NAME`. For v2, functions are backed by Cloud Run services — you can view them in the Cloud Run console. Traffic splitting lets you canary new versions: deploy, then shift traffic gradually.

scripts.description: Deploy an HTTP or event-driven Cloud Function (v2) with configurable runtime, memory, and triggers
